label: Locale
title: Locale
lead: Generate item, liquid, and manual locale keys for your mod.
bodyHtml:
What CUCoreLib localizes for you

CUCoreLib can collect locale text from the names, descriptions, and labels you write in code and write them into a single EN.json starter file. Item names and descriptions come from ItemRegistry.Register. Building names and descriptions come from BuildingEntityRegistry.Register. Liquid names and descriptions come from LiquidRegistry.Register. UI and other needed keys can be declared with LocaleRegistry.Require or resolved directly with LocaleRegistry.Get.

That means your mod can stay English-only in code, generate one baseline file, hand that file to a translator, and then ship a matching locale file like zh-CN.json for automatic runtime replacement.

Some autogenerated locale types:
SourceGenerated keyValue
ItemRegistry.Register("clothpatch", ...)item.clothpatchfullName
ItemRegistry.Register("clothpatch", ...)item.clothpatchdscdescription
BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("glassworkscentrifuge", ...)building.glassworkscentrifugeName
BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("glassworkscentrifuge", ...)building.glassworkscentrifugedscDescription
LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", ...)other.pineapplejuicename
LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", ...)other.pineapplejuicedscdescription
LocaleRegistry.Get("glassworks.menu.title", "Glassworks")other.glassworks.menu.titlefallback string

Manual keys and ad hoc lookups

... ad hoc means "as needed"...

For UI text, alerts, or other strings that do not come from registered items or liquids, (e.g. ui code, console command autofill) use LocaleRegistry.Get.


      string heatTooltip = LocaleRegistry.Get("other", "glassblowing.tooltip.heat", "Hot enough to warp a glove.");
      // Edit EN.json to add the "other.glassblowing.tooltip.heat" key with your desired translation, and the game will use that instead of the fallback string at runtime.
      // Fallback strings are optional, and not recommended to use

createLocale

Run createLocale in the in-game console after your mod registers its content. CUCoreLib writes or updates BepInEx/config/CUCoreLib/Locales/EN.json. You can pass a path if you want to write somewhere else.

createLocale
createLocale C:/Temp/EN.json

Existing user-written values are preserved for manual required keys. Generated item, building, liquid, and ad hoc keys are refreshed from the current registered names and descriptions, so changing fullName, description, Name, Description, name, or liquid description updates the file on the next run.

Generated shape

The JSON is grouped by the same categories used by vanilla locale lookup: item, building, moodle, and other. A translator can rename EN.json to something like zh-CN.json and replace the values without changing the keys.

Check the right side code for for a example LOLCAT translation ^w^

{
  "item": {
    "clothpatch": "Ruined yarn ball",
    "clothpatchdsc": "Why'd they have to ruin the yarn and make this?",
  },
  "building": {
    "glassworkscentrifuge": "Centrifuge",
    "glassworkscentrifugedsc": "A glassworks machine for separating liquids from containers."
  },
  "other": {
    "glassworks.menu.title": "Glassworks",
    "glassworks.tooltip.heat": "Hot enough to warp a glove."
  },
  "moodle": {
    "leadpoisoning": "Lead Poisoning",
    "leadpoisoningdsc": "Your body is having a very bad time."
  }
}
bodyText: What CUCoreLib localizes for you CUCoreLib can collect locale text from the names, descriptions, and labels you write in code and write them into a single EN.json starter file. Item names and descriptions come from ItemRegistry.Register . Building names and descriptions come from BuildingEntityRegistry.Register . Liquid names and descriptions come from LiquidRegistry.Register . UI and other needed keys can be declared with LocaleRegistry.Require or resolved directly with LocaleRegistry.Get . That means your mod can stay English-only in code, generate one baseline file, hand that file to a translator, and then ship a matching locale file like zh-CN.json for automatic runtime replacement. Some autogenerated locale types: Source Generated key Value ItemRegistry.Register("clothpatch", ...) item.clothpatch fullName ItemRegistry.Register("clothpatch", ...) item.clothpatchdsc description BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("glassworkscentrifuge", ...) building.glassworkscentrifuge Name BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("glassworkscentrifuge", ...) building.glassworkscentrifugedsc Description LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", ...) other.pineapplejuice name LiquidRegistry.Register("pineapplejuice", ...) other.pineapplejuicedsc description LocaleRegistry.Get("glassworks.menu.title", "Glassworks") other.glassworks.menu.title fallback string Manual keys and ad hoc lookups ... ad hoc means "as needed"... For UI text, alerts, or other strings that do not come from registered items or liquids, (e.g. ui code, console command autofill) use LocaleRegistry.Get . string heatTooltip = LocaleRegistry.Get("other", "glassblowing.tooltip.heat", "Hot enough to warp a glove."); // Edit EN.json to add the "other.glassblowing.tooltip.heat" key with your desired translation, and the game will use that instead of the fallback string at runtime. // Fallback strings are optional, and not recommended to use createLocale Run createLocale in the in-game console after your mod registers its content. CUCoreLib writes or updates BepInEx/config/CUCoreLib/Locales/EN.json . You can pass a path if you want to write somewhere else. createLocale createLocale C:/Temp/EN.json Existing user-written values are preserved for manual required keys. Generated item, building, liquid, and ad hoc keys are refreshed from the current registered names and descriptions, so changing fullName , description , Name , Description , name , or liquid description updates the file on the next run. Generated shape The JSON is grouped by the same categories used by vanilla locale lookup: item , building , moodle , and other . A translator can rename EN.json to something like zh-CN.json and replace the values without changing the keys. Check the right side code for for a example LOLCAT translation ^w^ { "item": { "clothpatch": "Ruined yarn ball", "clothpatchdsc": "Why'd they have to ruin the yarn and make this?", }, "building": { "glassworkscentrifuge": "Centrifuge", "glassworkscentrifugedsc": "A glassworks machine for separating liquids from containers." }, "other": { "glassworks.menu.title": "Glassworks", "glassworks.tooltip.heat": "Hot enough to warp a glove." }, "moodle": { "leadpoisoning": "Lead Poisoning", "leadpoisoningdsc": "Your body is having a very bad time." } }
code:
{
  "item": {
    "glass": "Glass",
    "glassdsc": "A werkable peece ob glass.",
    "conicalFlask": "Conical flask",
    "conicalFlaskdsc": "A simple glass flask fer careful mixin'.",
    "cinderstalk": "Cinderstalk",
    "cinderstalkdsc": "A tough, fibrous stalk blackened by heat. Not tasty like cheezburger.",
    "charredfern": "Charred Fern",
    "charredferndsc": "Brittle leaves dat crumble eazily. Smells like ash.",
    "clothpatch": "Ruined yarn ball",
    "clothpatchdsc": "Why'd they have to ruin the yarn and make this?",
    "reliefinjector": "Relief injector",
    "reliefinjectordsc": "A smol injector prefilled wif relief cream.",
    "cinderstalkbag": "Cinderstalk Bag",
    "cinderstalkbagdsc": "A compact all-natural bag. Who says plants can't be stylish?",
    "portablelamp": "Portable lamp",
    "portablelampdsc": "A battery-fed lamp fer da fuzzy cave explorer.",
    "glassshiv": "Glass shiv",
    "glassshivdsc": "This cuts my fur too easily...",
    "fieldpack": "Field Pack",
    "fieldpackdsc": "A versatile pack fer carryin' all the inner workings of a cat!."
  },
  "building": {
    "glasskiln": "Glass kiln",
    "glasskilndsc": "A smol kiln fer careful glasswork.",
    "glassworkscentrifuge": "Centrifuge",
    "glassworkscentrifugedsc": "I don't like the noise it makes..."
  },
  "moodle": {},
  "other": {
    "pineapplejuice": "Pineapple Juice",
    "pineapplejuicedsc": "Yoo've never seen this liquid before. A light yellow drink dat smells fruity. Yum yum!",
    "gamesetglassworks.audio.clinkvolume": "Glass clink volume",
    "gamesetglassworks.audio.clinkvolumedsc": "Protects your furry ears from clitter-clatter noises.",
    "galena": "Galena"
  }
}
